reason: Enabling the dracut FIPS module changes the system's cryptographic mode and requires a reboot. On an already installed RHEL 9 system, enabling FIPS is not supported and can render the system unbootable, causing loss of access. This aligns with the policy that FIPS changes are marked too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; Potential boot failure; Loss of SSH/console access; System instability; Compliance violation if not properly applied
